methodsAttempted.query: site:buffalo.edu University at Buffalo generative AI policy ChatGPT academic integrity teaching guidance
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official Academic Integrity, CATT, and UBNow candidates about AI use, syllabi, and academic integrity.

methodsAttempted.query: site:buffalo.edu University at Buffalo AI tools approved data privacy generative AI security
methodsAttempted.notes: Firecrawl search returned official UBIT guidance, UBIT safe-computing AI tools page, and the UBIT DeepSeek ban notice.

methodsAttempted.query: site:buffalo.edu University at Buffalo artificial intelligence research guidance IRB generative AI
methodsAttempted.notes: Research-oriented search primarily returned Empire AI showcases, research news, and a department-specific psychology milestone-project page rather than central research policy guidance.
Verified official University at Buffalo domains and subpaths including www.buffalo.edu/academic-integrity, www.buffalo.edu/catt, www.buffalo.edu/ubit, and official UB News/UBIT pages.

artifacts.summary: Official-source discovery found policy-specific UB sources from the Office of Academic Integrity, CATT, and UBIT. The extracted claims are scoped to those sources: UB states it has no universal student AI-use policy, instructors set course expectations, UBIT provides data-handling guidance for Gen AI, UB lists supported AI tools, and UB aligns with SUNY/state restrictions on DeepSeek. Aggregator, literacy, showcase, and department-specific pages were not used for central university policy claims.

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
AI Guidance for Syllabi and Assignments
Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology at the University at Buffalo
DeepSeek AI SUNY & state ban: What it means for UB

artifacts.claimText: UB's Office of Academic Integrity states that UB has no universal policy about student use of artificial intelligence and that instructors have academic freedom to decide which tools students may use for course learning objectives.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Academic Integrity

artifacts.claimText: Because UB has no universal AI policy for student use, the Office of Academic Integrity says instructors need to tell students what AI use is and is not allowed in the course or on each assessment, including in the syllabus.

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Academic Integrity

artifacts.claimText: UB CATT encourages faculty to add generative-AI statements to syllabi, avoid vague language, state specific parameters or limits, and distinguish permitted non-generative tools from generative AI tools.

citation.citationTitle: AI Guidance for Syllabi and Assignments
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Curriculum, Assessment and Teaching Transformation

artifacts.claimText: UBIT guidance says individuals should not enter Category 1 or Category 2 data, including non-public research data, into Gen AI tools that the university has not contracted with.

citation.citationTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology at the University at Buffalo

artifacts.claimText: UBIT's public AI page lists Microsoft Copilot Chat as UB-offered generative-AI software and lists Zoom AI Companion and Top Hat Ace as UB-offered software with AI-enhanced features.

A UBIT advisory says UB aligns with SUNY and New York State restrictions on DeepSeek AI, prohibiting its download, access, or use on UB-owned devices or UB networks, and says all AI tools must be reviewed and approved by the UB Software Review team.

citation.citationTitle: DeepSeek AI SUNY & state ban: What it means for UB

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: UB has no universal policy about student use of artificial intelligence. Instructors have the academic freedom to determine what tools students can and cannot use in pursuit of meeting course learning objectives.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Does UB have a policy about artificial intelligence use?

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Academic Integrity

artifacts.sourceTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Because there is no universal UB policy about artificial intelligence tools, instructors need to give students clear guidance about what is and is not allowed in their course overall and/or on each assessment.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: What should I tell my students about using artificial intelligence?

citation.citationTitle: Artificial Intelligence Guidance - Office of Academic Integrity
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Academic Integrity

artifacts.sourceTitle: AI Guidance for Syllabi and Assignments
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: Faculty are encouraged to add a statement regarding the use of Generative AI tools, such as ChatGPT. When communicating expectations, faculty should avoid using any vague language, include specific parameters or limits, and engage in class discussion around which (if any) Generative AI tools students can use.

citation.citationTitle: AI Guidance for Syllabi and Assignments
citation.publisher: University at Buffalo Office of Curriculum, Assessment and Teaching Transformation

artifacts.sourceTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology at the University at Buffalo
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: When using Gen AI technology individuals should not enter Category 1 or Category 2 data, including non-public research data into Gen AI tools of which the university has not entered into a contract with.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: Guidance Statement / Responsible Use of Generative AI Technology / Data Privacy

citation.citationTitle: Using Generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) Technology at the University at Buffalo

artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: UB-offered software with Generative AI: Microsoft Copilot Chat. UB-offered software with AI-enhanced features: Zoom AI Companion; Top Hat.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: UB-offered software with Generative AI / UB-offered software with AI-enhanced features

artifacts.sourceTitle: DeepSeek AI SUNY & state ban: What it means for UB
artifacts.evidenceSnippetOriginal: UB students, faculty and staff cannot download, access, or use DeepSeek AI on any UB-owned devices or when connected to UB networks. All AI tools, whether free or paid, must be reviewed and approved by the UB Software Review team.
artifacts.evidenceLocator: UB aligns with SUNY and state guidance / What to remember with AI at UB

citation.citationTitle: DeepSeek AI SUNY & state ban: What it means for UB

artifacts.notes: Approved as a narrow Academic Integrity claim. Source expressly says no universal UB student AI-use policy and gives instructors course-level discretion.
Approved as teaching/academic-integrity guidance, not a central prohibition or approval of any AI use.
Approved as CATT guidance to faculty on drafting syllabus and assignment expectations. Example prohibition language was not converted into a university-wide ban.
Approved with mandatory wording limited to the source's should-not data-handling language for Category 1/2 data and non-contracted Gen AI tools.
Approved as a tool availability/treatment claim. It does not imply these tools are approved for every course or data type.
Approved as a security-review and access-restriction claim because the UBIT advisory uses cannot/must language for DeepSeek and software review.

artifacts.title: University at Buffalo SUNY AI Policy Staged Crawl
artifacts.summary: The staged crawl found no central UB policy universally governing student use of AI. Official UB sources instead say instructors set course expectations; CATT provides syllabus guidance; UBIT gives Gen AI data-handling guidance and lists UB-offered AI tools; and a UBIT advisory states UB aligns with SUNY/state DeepSeek restrictions and requires AI tools to be reviewed by the UB Software Review team.
limitations: This tracker is not legal advice, not academic integrity advice, and not an official university statement unless a linked source is the university's own official page.
No central binding UB policy universally allowing or prohibiting student AI use was found in the official sources reviewed.
Department-specific, aggregator, literacy, and research-showcase pages were not used to create university-wide claims.
artifacts.sourceRightsPolicy: Tracker metadata is open licensed. Official source documents, page text, PDFs, and other source materials retain their original rights and terms.
